prompt: Cybernetic translucent bioluminescent flower concept infographic, 9:16 vertical version. In the center is a hyper-realistic 3D cyber-flower, suspended in a cold black futuristic laboratory void. Petals and leaves have a translucent frosted glass texture, with subsurface scattering, X-ray transparency, thin-film refraction, and microscopic water droplets; internal plant veins are transformed into PCB circuits, optical fibers, luminescent capillaries, and energy fluids. Maintain a real plant structure: 65% organic lifeform, 35% digital circuitry. Adopt a "star-satellite" composition: the central 3D flower occupies about 30%, while the periphery 70% is a high-density 2D HUD information system. 10 independent data modules are accurately arranged at the edge of the screen, strictly aligned to a hidden grid, connected to petals, leaf veins, stems, stamens, and circuit nodes with 0.5pt ultra-fine straight lines and 90-degree mechanical folded lines. Modules embed miniature bar charts, waveforms, spectral curves, contour profiles, topological nodes, and dot matrix scales. Visual style: Hyper-realistic biological X-ray rendering × cold orthogonal HUD interface × futuristic plant experimental archives. Background is an infinite void close to black, with extremely weak digital grain, scan lines, dot matrix grids, and volumetric fog. The subject integrates naturally into the background without a frame, and the peripheral UI adopts hard-edged modular cutting. Avoid ordinary game interfaces, cheap cyberpunk, cartoon textures, and random mechanical parts collage. **Color Theme: Tulip*** Background Base: cold black* Translucent Structural Color: {low saturation glass color}* Main Bio-fluorescence: {main color}* Auxiliary Fluorescence: {auxiliary color}* Single Accent Color: {accent color}* Technical Text Color: {text color}Color rules: Background and structural color occupy about 60%, main and auxiliary fluorescence occupy about 30%, and the accent color occupies only 5%-10%. A maximum of two fluorescent colors and one accent color per screen. The accent color is strictly used for stamen energy cores, circuit nodes, key arrows, danger parameters, and data peaks; large-area use and random rainbow gradients are prohibited. Font adopts condensed technical document typography. A super-large pure Chinese bold title is set at the top, about 64px; Pinyin, English, and mixed Chinese-English are prohibited. 10 modules use monospaced subtitles, technical sans-serif body text, and high-density micro-text. Text becomes denser from the center to the edges, does not block the subject, and no garbled characters appear. Subject color mapping: Petals use transparent structural colors, petal edges and leaf veins use the main fluorescent color, internal fine veins and optical fibers use the auxiliary fluorescent color, and PCB intersection nodes and stamen cores only use the accent color. Peripheral HUD brightness must not exceed the central flower. High detail, cinematic lighting, real refraction, volumetric glow, sharp edges, precision layout, print-grade quality, unified composition, extremely high information density. Subject: tulip
